metadata.note: Output used as-is without manual revision or selection.

causal_texts: A sudden storm damaged the old bridge across the river. Engineers closed the old bridge to all traffic. Workers repaired the old bridge with new steel beams. Cars and trucks crossed the old bridge without any trouble.

A fierce gale cracked the lantern glass of the small lighthouse. The keeper reported the damage at the small lighthouse to the harbor office. A repair crew fitted new glass panels in the small lighthouse. The small lighthouse warned passing ships away from the rocky coast.

A collector bought the wooden sailboat at a small coastal auction. Two carpenters replaced the rotten planks of the wooden sailboat. A crane lowered the wooden sailboat into the calm harbor water. A young crew sailed the wooden sailboat across the open bay.

An old baker sold the village bakery to a young couple. Builders rebuilt the stone oven inside the village bakery. The village bakery opened with fresh bread and long morning queues. Hungry neighbors filled the village bakery every single day.

A dealer discovered the antique clock in a dusty attic. A watchmaker cleaned every brass wheel inside the antique clock. A collector bought the antique clock for a large sum. The antique clock chimed each hour in a quiet hallway.

Heavy rain washed away part of the mountain trail. Rangers closed the mountain trail to all hikers. Volunteers rebuilt the mountain trail with stone steps and gravel. Hikers climbed the mountain trail to the summit without trouble.

A traveler bought the copper kettle at a crowded market stall. Years of hard use dented the side of the copper kettle. A tinsmith hammered the copper kettle back into a smooth shape. The copper kettle boiled water for tea every morning.

Lightning split a heavy branch from the old oak tree. Arborists cut away the broken wood from the old oak tree. New green shoots covered the scarred side of the old oak tree. Children climbed the old oak tree during long warm afternoons.

A storm tore the canvas sails from the stone windmill. The owner ordered new sails for the stone windmill. Millwrights fitted the new sails onto the stone windmill. The stone windmill ground wheat into flour for local bakers.

A burst pipe flooded the basement of the city library. Staff moved thousands of wet books out of the city library. Restorers dried and repaired the damaged books from the city library. Readers returned to the city library in large numbers.

A carpenter built the leather armchair from oak and dark hide. A family used the leather armchair beside a warm fireplace. Long years of use cracked the seat of the leather armchair. A restorer covered the leather armchair with fresh brown leather.

An optician built the brass telescope for a wealthy merchant. Salt air tarnished every surface of the brass telescope. A specialist polished the brass telescope back to a bright shine. A museum displayed the brass telescope in a tall glass case.

A sharp rise in rent threatened the corner bookshop. Loyal customers raised money for the corner bookshop. The landlord lowered the rent for the corner bookshop. The corner bookshop sold books to a new generation of readers.

Miners cut limestone from the abandoned quarry for many decades. Falling demand emptied the abandoned quarry of workers and machines. Rain filled the deep pit of the abandoned quarry with clear water. Swimmers and divers visited the abandoned quarry every summer.

A farmer bought the red tractor from a small machinery dealer. Mud and rain rusted the wheel arches of the red tractor. A mechanic replaced the tired engine of the red tractor. The red tractor pulled a heavy plough across the wet fields.

A shipyard built the harbor ferry from riveted steel plates. Thousands of passengers crossed the bay on the harbor ferry. An engine fire stopped the harbor ferry in the narrow channel. A tugboat towed the harbor ferry back to the quay.

A music teacher founded the school orchestra with twelve young players. Weekly rehearsals sharpened the sound of the school orchestra. A regional competition invited the school orchestra to a concert hall. The school orchestra won the top prize at a national final.

A gardener built the glass greenhouse against a warm brick wall. Hail smashed several panes of the glass greenhouse. A glazier replaced the broken panes of the glass greenhouse. Tomatoes and peppers ripened inside the glass greenhouse.

A sculptor carved the marble fountain for a wealthy local family. Frost cracked the wide basin of the marble fountain. Masons repaired the marble fountain with new blocks of stone. Water flowed through the marble fountain in the town square.

Fire destroyed the wooden roof of the old cathedral. Architects surveyed the damaged walls of the old cathedral. Stonemasons rebuilt the old cathedral with limestone from a nearby quarry. Pilgrims and tourists entered the old cathedral in great numbers.

Winter storms weakened the wooden pier along the seafront. The council closed the wooden pier for urgent inspection. Divers replaced the rotten piles under the wooden pier. Families walked along the wooden pier in the summer sunshine.

Engineers built the coastal railway along a line of steep cliffs. A landslide buried a long section of the coastal railway. Work crews cleared the heavy rubble from the coastal railway. Trains carried holidaymakers along the coastal railway to the harbor town.

A great grandfather built the family farmhouse from local grey stone. Damp weakened the thick walls of the family farmhouse. Builders installed a new roof and dry floors in the family farmhouse. Three generations gathered in the family farmhouse for a long celebration.

Engineers laid the desert highway across a wide plain of sand. Drifting dunes buried long stretches of the desert highway. Bulldozers cleared the deep sand from the desert highway. Trucks crossed the desert highway with fuel and fresh food.

A wealthy donor gave a large collection to the town museum. Curators arranged the new objects inside the town museum. A regional newspaper praised the town museum in a long article. Visitors queued outside the town museum every weekend.

A blacksmith forged the iron gate for a grand country house. Rain and salt rusted the hinges of the iron gate. A restorer sanded and painted the iron gate. The iron gate swung open for a stream of garden visitors.

A jeweler made the silver locket for a young bride. A careless fall dented the front of the silver locket. A craftsman smoothed the dent from the silver locket. A granddaughter wore the silver locket at a family wedding.

Two brothers built the mountain cabin from rough spruce logs. Deep snow crushed the roof of the mountain cabin. A carpenter rebuilt the roof of the mountain cabin with stronger beams. Climbers sheltered inside the mountain cabin during a fierce blizzard.

A shipwright built the fishing boat from oak and larch. A crew hauled cod and herring aboard the fishing boat for years. A rough winter sea cracked the hull of the fishing boat. A yard rebuilt the fishing boat with new planks and paint.

A cracked pipe drained the water from the public swimming pool. The council closed the public swimming pool for major repairs. Contractors relined the public swimming pool with fresh blue tiles. Local children swam in the public swimming pool every holiday.

A local company opened the old cinema in a former theater. Falling ticket sales emptied the seats of the old cinema. A film society took over the old cinema. Crowds packed the old cinema for classic movie nights.

A student left the rusty bicycle in a damp shed for years. Rain and neglect seized the chain of the rusty bicycle. A repair shop rebuilt the rusty bicycle with new parts. A courier rode the rusty bicycle across the city every day.

A farmer sprayed the wildflower meadow with strong chemicals. Poppies and orchids vanished from the wildflower meadow. A conservation group bought the wildflower meadow at auction. Bees and butterflies returned to the wildflower meadow in great numbers.

Woodworm weakened the roof beams of the village church. The parish raised money for the village church at many concerts. Carpenters replaced the damaged beams in the village church. A large choir filled the village church for a candlelit concert.

Neighbors cleared rubble and weeds from the community garden. Volunteers built long raised beds inside the community garden. Fresh vegetables grew across every corner of the community garden. A hundred families shared the harvest from the community garden.

An architect designed the concert hall with curved wooden walls. Musicians complained about the poor acoustics of the concert hall. Engineers hung new panels above the stage of the concert hall. A famous orchestra recorded a symphony in the concert hall.

Heavy rain swelled the winding river beyond the usual banks. Floodwater from the winding river covered nearby fields and roads. Engineers built new embankments along the winding river. Anglers fished the winding river in perfect peace.

A wealthy merchant founded the paper mill beside a fast stream. The paper mill supplied paper to printers across the whole region. Cheap imports from abroad closed the paper mill for good. A developer converted the paper mill into artist studios.

A factory in Vienna built the grand piano from spruce and maple. Damp air warped the soundboard of the grand piano. A technician rebuilt the grand piano over several months. A pianist played a Chopin nocturne on the grand piano.

Monks founded the ancient monastery on a windy limestone plateau. Raiders burned the library of the ancient monastery. Stonemasons restored the ancient monastery with thick new walls. Scholars studied rare manuscripts inside the ancient monastery.

Two governments opened the border checkpoint on a mountain road. Long queues of trucks blocked the border checkpoint. Officials added extra lanes at the border checkpoint. Trucks and cars moved quickly through the border checkpoint.

Engineers cut the mountain railway into a steep granite slope. An avalanche destroyed a bridge on the mountain railway. Workers rebuilt the damaged bridge on the mountain railway. Tourists rode the mountain railway to a high alpine hotel.

Warm water bleached large areas of the coral reef. Fish stocks fell sharply all around the coral reef. Marine biologists planted new coral fragments on the coral reef. Colorful fish crowded the coral reef in growing numbers.

A family planted the small vineyard on a warm southern slope. A hard frost killed many young vines in the small vineyard. The family replanted the small vineyard with hardier stock. The small vineyard produced a crisp white wine of high quality.

A farmer raised the wooden barn with help from many neighbors. A winter gale ripped several boards from the wooden barn. Carpenters patched the wooden barn with fresh cut oak. Fresh hay and heavy machinery filled the wooden barn.

The town built the fire station beside the main road. Growing traffic delayed the crews of the fire station. Planners opened a wide new exit at the fire station. Engines left the fire station without any delay.

A potter shaped the clay pot on a spinning wheel. A hot kiln hardened the clay pot into red stoneware. A careless move chipped the rim of the clay pot. A gardener planted a small lemon tree in the clay pot.

A weaver made the woolen blanket on an old hand loom. Moths chewed several holes in the woolen blanket. A seamstress darned the woolen blanket with matching thread. A grandchild wrapped up in the woolen blanket on cold evenings.

A printer founded the local newspaper in a small back room. The local newspaper reported on farms, schools, and council meetings. Online competition cut the sales of the local newspaper. A group of readers bought the local newspaper as a cooperative.

A Greek family opened the corner diner beside a busy junction. Truck drivers filled the corner diner throughout the night. A kitchen fire closed the corner diner for many months. A full refurbishment brought the crowds back to the corner diner.

A shepherd built the stone cottage from rough granite blocks. Decades of neglect opened the roof of the stone cottage. A young couple restored the stone cottage with local slate. Guests rented the stone cottage throughout the walking season.

Ancient rivers laid down the layers of the sandstone cliff. Wind and rain carved deep hollows into the sandstone cliff. A large rockfall closed the beach below the sandstone cliff. Climbers avoided the sandstone cliff for many years.

The city opened the ice rink in a former tram depot. A failing compressor melted the surface of the ice rink. Engineers installed new cooling pipes under the ice rink. Skating clubs trained on the ice rink every evening.

A university founded the botanical garden on the edge of the city. Budget cuts closed several glasshouses in the botanical garden. A charity raised funds for the botanical garden. Rare tropical plants filled the warm glasshouses of the botanical garden.

A journalist bought the old typewriter from a city stationer. Thousands of pages wore down the letters of the old typewriter. A repairer fitted new type bars in the old typewriter. A novelist wrote an entire book on the old typewriter.

Engineers erected the harbor crane on a broad concrete quay. The harbor crane lifted timber and coal for many decades. Modern container ships made the harbor crane completely useless. The city preserved the harbor crane as an industrial monument.

A retreating glacier carved the deep basin of the mountain lake. Meltwater filled the mountain lake with clear cold water. A hydro scheme lowered the level of the mountain lake. Hikers camped on the wide gravel shores of the mountain lake.

Navvies built the canal lock from brick and heavy oak gates. Constant use rotted the gates of the canal lock. A restoration trust replaced the gates of the canal lock. Narrowboats passed through the canal lock every summer day.

Villagers dug the village well through twenty meters of chalk. The village well supplied drinking water to every household. A new pipeline made the village well unnecessary. A local society restored the village well as a monument.

A Baltic craftsman strung the amber necklace from polished golden beads. A merchant carried the amber necklace to a southern city. A noble family kept the amber necklace in a locked cabinet. A museum displayed the amber necklace among ancient jewelry from many lands.

Two brothers founded the traveling circus with a single striped tent. The traveling circus toured hundreds of small towns every summer. Rising fuel and insurance costs almost ruined the traveling circus. A new acrobatic show saved the traveling circus.

Scientists built the weather station on a remote windswept ridge. A thick coat of ice damaged the instruments of the weather station. A helicopter brought new equipment to the weather station. The weather station sent accurate data to forecasters across the country.

Builders raised the observatory dome above a high mountain ridge. A jammed motor froze the observatory dome in one position. Technicians replaced the drive motors of the observatory dome. Astronomers photographed distant galaxies from the observatory dome.

A mining company assembled the freight train from sixty steel wagons. The freight train hauled iron ore across a dry plateau. A broken axle derailed the freight train near a small station. A heavy crane lifted the freight train back onto the rails.

Farmers drained part of the salt marsh for new pasture. Rising seas flooded the low fields beside the salt marsh. A conservation body returned the ruined fields to the salt marsh. Thousands of wading birds fed on the salt marsh.

Prospectors opened the copper mine in a bare rocky valley. Miners cut deep tunnels through the rock of the copper mine. A collapse in metal prices closed the copper mine. A heritage railway carried visitors into the copper mine.

Masons built the town clock tower above the old market hall. A lightning strike stopped the mechanism in the town clock tower. A specialist firm repaired the mechanism of the town clock tower. The town clock tower struck the hour across the whole town.

A craftsman built the wooden canoe from thin cedar strips. A sharp rock tore a hole in the wooden canoe. A friend patched the wooden canoe with fresh resin and cloth. Two paddlers crossed a wide lake in the wooden canoe.

Monks planted the herb garden beside a stone infirmary. Weeds and brambles swallowed the herb garden over many years. Volunteers cleared the brambles and replanted the herb garden. Cooks gathered fresh green leaves from the herb garden.

The town laid the seaside promenade along a curving pebble beach. Winter storms broke the iron railings of the seaside promenade. Contractors rebuilt the seaside promenade with stronger concrete. Families strolled along the seaside promenade every warm evening.

A sculptor carved the granite statue from a single block of stone. Vandals broke the outstretched arm of the granite statue. A conservator repaired the arm of the granite statue. Tourists photographed the granite statue in the main square.

Builders added the school gymnasium behind the main teaching block. A leaking roof warped the floor of the school gymnasium. Contractors laid a new maple floor in the school gymnasium. Basketball teams played every home match in the school gymnasium.

A resort company installed the ski lift on a north facing slope. A heavy storm twisted two pylons of the ski lift. Engineers rebuilt the damaged pylons of the ski lift. Skiers rode the ski lift to the highest run.

A young couple opened the pottery workshop in a former stable. A cracked kiln stopped all production at the pottery workshop. A grant paid for a new kiln at the pottery workshop. Students filled every evening class at the pottery workshop.

Herring boats brought wealth to the fishing village for generations. Overfishing emptied the sea around the fishing village. Young families left the fishing village for city work. Summer tourists filled the empty cottages of the fishing village.

A miller built the flour mill beside a fast flowing stream. A flood swept away the waterwheel of the flour mill. Engineers fitted a steam engine in the flour mill. The flour mill supplied bakers across the whole county.

A family founded the puppet theater in a narrow city street. Generations of children watched shows at the puppet theater. A fire destroyed the workshop of the puppet theater. Craftspeople carved hundreds of new puppets for the puppet theater.

Engineers erected the radio tower on a bare chalk hill. The radio tower carried broadcasts to half of the region. Ice brought down a guy wire of the radio tower. A specialist crew climbed the radio tower for urgent repairs.

A farmer planted the apple orchard on a gentle southern slope. A hard frost killed much of the blossom in the apple orchard. Warm rain rescued a small crop in the apple orchard. Pickers filled a hundred crates from the apple orchard.

A charity founded the children's hospital in a converted mansion. Growing demand overwhelmed the wards of the children's hospital. A huge donation funded a new wing at the children's hospital. Young patients recovered in the bright rooms of the children's hospital.

Masons cut the stone staircase into a steep cliff face. Centuries of feet wore deep hollows in the stone staircase. Engineers fixed iron handrails along the stone staircase. Pilgrims climbed the stone staircase to a small chapel.

A carpenter built the beach hut from planks and salvaged timber. Salt wind stripped the paint from the beach hut. An owner painted the beach hut a bright turquoise. Two families shared tea and shelter in the beach hut.

An artist opened the sculpture park on an old country estate. Storms toppled several tall works in the sculpture park. Engineers anchored the sculptures in the sculpture park with concrete bases. Coaches brought school groups to the sculpture park.

A merchant founded the glass factory beside a bed of white sand. Skilled blowers produced fine bottles at the glass factory. Cheap foreign imports closed the glass factory for good. A museum opened inside the old halls of the glass factory.

A father built the toy train set on a wide plywood board. Careless hands broke the small station of the toy train set. A grandfather rebuilt the missing pieces of the toy train set. Grandchildren played with the toy train set for hours.

An architect designed the university chapel in a plain modern style. Damp ruined the pale plaster inside the university chapel. Restorers dried and replastered the walls of the university chapel. A choir sang evening services in the university chapel.

Traders crossed the mountain pass with mules and heavy loads. Engineers built a paved road over the mountain pass. Avalanches closed the mountain pass for many weeks. A long tunnel replaced the road across the mountain pass.

Roman craftsmen laid the ancient mosaic in a wealthy villa. Collapsing walls buried the ancient mosaic under rubble and soil. A farmer's plough uncovered a corner of the ancient mosaic. Archaeologists cleaned and recorded the ancient mosaic with great care.

Two friends opened the record shop in a damp basement. Streaming services cut the sales of the record shop. A vinyl revival filled the record shop with new customers. The record shop expanded into the empty floor above.

Mosses formed the peat bog over thousands of wet years. Farmers cut fuel from the peat bog for many generations. Drainage ditches dried the surface of the peat bog. A conservation trust blocked the ditches and rewetted the peat bog.

Monks founded a small chapel on the tidal island. Rising tides cut off the tidal island twice a day. The county built a raised causeway to the tidal island. Day visitors crowded the tidal island through every summer.

A small club started the folk festival in a village hall. Growing crowds moved the folk festival into a large field. Heavy rain turned the folk festival into a sea of mud. Organizers laid solid tracks across the site of the folk festival.

Merchants funded the customs house beside a busy stone quay. Officials weighed cargo and collected duty at the customs house. A change in trade rules emptied the customs house. A developer converted the customs house into expensive riverside apartments.

A utility built the wind farm on a windy coastal ridge. Local residents complained about the noise of the wind farm. Engineers replaced the blades of the wind farm with quieter designs. The wind farm supplied power to forty thousand homes.

The parish built the village school beside the old churchyard. Falling numbers threatened the village school with closure. New families brought a dozen young pupils to the village school. Children filled all three classrooms of the village school.

Soldiers built the old fortress on a rocky coastal headland. A long siege broke the outer walls of the old fortress. Powerful new artillery made the old fortress useless for defense. A trust opened the old fortress to summer visitors.

A farming family started the cheese dairy in a cool stone cellar. Strict new rules closed the cheese dairy for months. Builders fitted stainless steel tanks in the cheese dairy. The cheese dairy won a gold medal at a national show.

Rangers built the forest boardwalk across a soft peaty swamp. Damp rot weakened the planks of the forest boardwalk. Volunteers replaced the planks of the forest boardwalk with treated timber. School groups walked safely along the forest boardwalk.

A huge iron meteorite blasted the meteor crater into a desert plain. Wind and rain softened the rim of the meteor crater. Miners searched the meteor crater for valuable metal. A visitor center opened beside the meteor crater.

A railway company opened the historic hotel beside a new station. Wealthy travelers filled every room of the historic hotel. Two wars and changing tastes emptied the historic hotel. A careful restoration returned grandeur to the historic hotel.
noncausal_texts: The old bridge spanned a wide river with three stone arches. The old bridge carried a narrow road between two villages. The old bridge appeared on many postcards of the valley. The old bridge attracted photographers from distant towns.

The small lighthouse stood on a granite headland above the sea. The small lighthouse held a rotating lamp behind thick curved glass. The small lighthouse wore two broad red stripes on white stone. The small lighthouse belonged to the regional coastal authority.

The wooden sailboat carried a single tall mast and white sails. The wooden sailboat measured nine meters from bow to stern. The wooden sailboat slept four people in a narrow forward cabin. The wooden sailboat belonged to a family from the fishing town.

The village bakery occupied a low brick building beside the square. The village bakery baked rye loaves in a wide stone oven. The village bakery employed four people from the surrounding farms. The village bakery smelled of warm butter and burnt sugar.

The antique clock stood two meters tall in a walnut case. The antique clock chimed with a deep bronze bell. The antique clock showed the phases of the moon on a small dial. The antique clock came from a workshop in southern Germany.

The mountain trail climbed eight hundred meters through dense pine forest. The mountain trail crossed two cold streams on wooden planks. The mountain trail offered wide views of the northern valley. The mountain trail carried red paint marks on grey rocks.

The copper kettle held two liters of water without any leak. The copper kettle wore a black wooden handle above a rounded lid. The copper kettle sat on a cast iron stove in the kitchen. The copper kettle bore the mark of a Dutch workshop.

The old oak tree stood alone in the middle of a wide meadow. The old oak tree spread branches over twenty meters of grass. The old oak tree sheltered a colony of noisy rooks. The old oak tree marked the boundary of two old farms.

The stone windmill rose four floors above a flat green field. The stone windmill turned two heavy millstones with wooden gears. The stone windmill served farmers from the whole district. The stone windmill wore a white cap of painted timber.

The city library held four hundred thousand books on open shelves. The city library occupied a red brick building near the river. The city library ran free reading classes for young children. The city library kept a quiet study room under a glass roof.

The leather armchair stood on four carved oak legs. The leather armchair wore a deep brown hide with brass studs. The leather armchair filled a corner of a small reading room. The leather armchair carried the smell of old tobacco smoke.

The brass telescope measured one meter along a polished tube. The brass telescope stood on a heavy tripod of dark wood. The brass telescope showed the rings of Saturn on clear nights. The brass telescope came from a famous workshop in London.

The corner bookshop filled two floors of a narrow old house. The corner bookshop specialized in poetry and travel writing. The corner bookshop kept a fat grey cat near the till. The corner bookshop hosted readings in a small back room.

The abandoned quarry cut a wide bowl into a chalk hillside. The abandoned quarry held a deep lake of green water. The abandoned quarry sheltered rare orchids on the warm southern ledges. The abandoned quarry lay two kilometers from the nearest village.

The red tractor carried a narrow cab with a cracked rear window. The red tractor ran on a four cylinder diesel engine. The red tractor stood in a corrugated shed beside the barn. The red tractor bore the name of an Italian manufacturer.

The harbor ferry carried two hundred passengers on two open decks. The harbor ferry crossed a narrow channel between two busy piers. The harbor ferry wore a blue hull with a white stripe. The harbor ferry served commuters and tourists throughout the year.

The school orchestra included forty players from three different year groups. The school orchestra rehearsed in a wide hall with tall windows. The school orchestra favored short pieces by living composers. The school orchestra borrowed instruments from the regional music service.

The glass greenhouse covered thirty square meters of a walled garden. The glass greenhouse rested on a low foundation of red brick. The glass greenhouse held long benches of clay pots and seed trays. The glass greenhouse trapped warm air under a steep sloping roof.

The marble fountain rose three meters above a paved square. The marble fountain showed four dolphins around a central column. The marble fountain drew water from an old underground spring. The marble fountain attracted pigeons and tired shoppers on warm days.

The old cathedral held two square towers above a broad western front. The old cathedral covered the graves of several medieval bishops. The old cathedral contained a rose window of deep blue glass. The old cathedral stood at the highest point of the town.

The wooden pier stretched two hundred meters into a shallow bay. The wooden pier carried a small cafe and a fishing platform. The wooden pier rested on hundreds of tarred timber piles. The wooden pier belonged to the seaside town council.

The coastal railway ran forty kilometers between two small harbor towns. The coastal railway passed through six short tunnels in the cliffs. The coastal railway used old diesel carriages with wide windows. The coastal railway offered clear views of the grey northern sea.

The family farmhouse contained six rooms under a steep slate roof. The family farmhouse stood at the end of a long gravel track. The family farmhouse looked across forty hectares of green pasture. The family farmhouse kept a deep well in the back courtyard.

The desert highway ran three hundred kilometers between two remote towns. The desert highway carried heavy trucks and occasional tourist buses. The desert highway passed a single fuel station near a dry riverbed. The desert highway shimmered under a fierce afternoon sun.

The town museum occupied a former courthouse beside the market square. The town museum displayed Roman coins and medieval farm tools. The town museum charged no entrance fee for local residents. The town museum employed two curators and a dozen volunteers.

The iron gate closed a wide gap in a tall brick wall. The iron gate showed a pattern of leaves and twisted bars. The iron gate weighed almost half a ton. The iron gate carried the crest of an old local family.

The silver locket held two small portraits behind an oval glass. The silver locket hung from a fine chain of braided silver. The silver locket carried an engraved pattern of ivy leaves. The silver locket weighed very little in an open palm.

The mountain cabin stood at eighteen hundred meters below a rocky ridge. The mountain cabin contained eight bunks and a cast iron stove. The mountain cabin belonged to a regional climbing club. The mountain cabin kept an emergency radio in a metal box.

The fishing boat measured twelve meters from bow to stern. The fishing boat carried green nets on a wide open deck. The fishing boat worked from a small harbor in the north. The fishing boat wore a blue hull and a white wheelhouse.

The public swimming pool measured twenty five meters along six lanes. The public swimming pool sat under a curved roof of pale concrete. The public swimming pool offered lessons for children and older adults. The public swimming pool smelled strongly of chlorine and wet tiles.

The old cinema held three hundred red velvet seats. The old cinema kept a plaster ceiling with painted gold stars. The old cinema showed classic films on a single wide screen. The old cinema stood on a busy street near the station.

The rusty bicycle carried a steel frame and a worn leather saddle. The rusty bicycle wore flakes of green paint over brown rust. The rusty bicycle held a wicker basket above the front wheel. The rusty bicycle came from a factory in central England.

The wildflower meadow covered six hectares beside a slow river. The wildflower meadow supported orchids, poppies, and tall yellow rattle. The wildflower meadow drew beekeepers from the surrounding villages. The wildflower meadow flooded gently in the wettest winters.

The village church held a square tower with four bronze bells. The village church contained oak pews from a distant century. The village church stood beside a graveyard of leaning stones. The village church served three small parishes in the valley.

The community garden filled a vacant lot between two apartment blocks. The community garden contained forty plots behind a wire fence. The community garden supplied vegetables to a nearby soup kitchen. The community garden kept a tool shed and a rainwater tank.

The concert hall seated two thousand people on three steep tiers. The concert hall stood beside a canal in the old harbor district. The concert hall carried an organ with four thousand pipes. The concert hall hosted jazz festivals and school graduations.

The winding river flowed ninety kilometers from a moorland spring to the sea. The winding river supported trout, otters, and grey herons. The winding river turned through wide loops across a flat valley. The winding river marked the border of two old counties.

The paper mill occupied a long brick building beside a weir. The paper mill drew power from a large iron waterwheel. The paper mill employed two hundred people from the valley. The paper mill produced fine paper for books and banknotes.

The grand piano stretched two and a half meters across a polished floor. The grand piano carried eighty eight keys of ivory and ebony. The grand piano rested on three heavy brass castors. The grand piano stood in the corner of a bright music room.

The ancient monastery enclosed a square cloister with a central well. The ancient monastery housed a library of illuminated manuscripts. The ancient monastery overlooked a deep gorge from a limestone plateau. The ancient monastery kept a small vineyard on the southern slope.

The border checkpoint sat at fourteen hundred meters in a narrow pass. The border checkpoint contained six lanes under a wide steel canopy. The border checkpoint operated day and night throughout the year. The border checkpoint employed officers from two neighboring countries.

The mountain railway climbed twelve hundred meters over eight kilometers of track. The mountain railway used a toothed rack between the rails. The mountain railway ran red carriages with large curved windows. The mountain railway ended at a station below a glacier.

The coral reef stretched forty kilometers along a shallow tropical shelf. The coral reef sheltered turtles, reef sharks, and many fish species. The coral reef protected a low island from heavy ocean swells. The coral reef drew divers from many distant countries.

The small vineyard covered four hectares on a steep chalk slope. The small vineyard grew riesling vines in long straight rows. The small vineyard belonged to a family from the nearby village. The small vineyard produced twelve thousand bottles of white wine.

The wooden barn rose two floors above a cobbled farmyard. The wooden barn stored hay and straw under a broad gambrel roof. The wooden barn wore dark red paint on rough pine boards. The wooden barn sheltered a colony of swallows every summer.

The fire station housed three engines behind wide red doors. The fire station kept a training tower in the rear yard. The fire station served a district of twenty thousand people. The fire station employed two shifts of full time crews.

The clay pot held about ten liters of dry soil. The clay pot wore a rough glaze of green and brown. The clay pot stood on a sunny terrace beside a stone wall. The clay pot carried the fingerprints of the potter near the base.

The woolen blanket measured two meters across a wide bed. The woolen blanket carried a pattern of red and grey squares. The woolen blanket came from a mill in the Scottish borders. The woolen blanket held a thick fringe along both short edges.

The local newspaper appeared every week on thin grey paper. The local newspaper covered a district of thirty villages. The local newspaper employed four reporters and one photographer. The local newspaper carried a famous column about garden birds.

The corner diner served pancakes and strong coffee all day. The corner diner kept twelve red stools along a chrome counter. The corner diner stood at a junction of two wide roads. The corner diner played old country songs from a battered jukebox.

The stone cottage contained two rooms under a low slate roof. The stone cottage stood beside a small stream on open moorland. The stone cottage kept thick walls of rough granite blocks. The stone cottage held a wide fireplace of blackened stone.

The sandstone cliff rose eighty meters above a narrow pebble beach. The sandstone cliff showed bands of red and yellow rock. The sandstone cliff sheltered nests of fulmars and jackdaws. The sandstone cliff attracted geology students from several universities.

The ice rink measured sixty meters along a wide oval track. The ice rink stood under a curved roof of old steel beams. The ice rink hosted hockey matches and public skating sessions. The ice rink kept a small cafe behind the northern barrier.

The botanical garden covered twenty hectares beside a slow green river. The botanical garden grew four thousand plant species in labeled beds. The botanical garden kept a palm house of curved iron and glass. The botanical garden trained gardeners from many parts of the world.

The old typewriter weighed nearly fifteen kilograms in a black steel case. The old typewriter carried round glass keys with white letters. The old typewriter used a two color ribbon of red and black. The old typewriter came from a large factory in Frankfurt.

The harbor crane rose thirty meters above a stone quay. The harbor crane ran on steel rails along the waterfront. The harbor crane lifted loads of up to ten tons. The harbor crane wore faded green paint over heavy rivets.

The mountain lake filled a rocky basin at two thousand meters. The mountain lake held cold water above a bed of pale gravel. The mountain lake reflected three sharp peaks on calm mornings. The mountain lake fed a stream toward the valley farms.

The canal lock raised boats three meters between two long pounds. The canal lock used oak gates with heavy iron beams. The canal lock sat beside a whitewashed cottage of the keeper. The canal lock formed part of a waterway across the moors.

The village well reached twenty meters through solid chalk. The village well stood in the middle of a small green. The village well carried a wooden roof above a stone rim. The village well held cold water throughout the driest summers.

The amber necklace held forty beads of warm golden resin. The amber necklace trapped a small insect inside one clear bead. The amber necklace came from the shores of the Baltic Sea. The amber necklace rested in a case of dark blue velvet.

The traveling circus carried a striped tent for eight hundred spectators. The traveling circus employed acrobats, clowns, and a small brass band. The traveling circus moved between towns in a convoy of trucks. The traveling circus used no animals in any performance.

The weather station stood at nine hundred meters on an exposed ridge. The weather station measured wind, rainfall, and air pressure every hour. The weather station ran on solar panels and a small wind turbine. The weather station sent data to a national forecasting center.

The observatory dome measured twelve meters across a circular concrete base. The observatory dome rotated on a ring of steel wheels. The observatory dome sheltered a telescope with a mirror of two meters. The observatory dome gleamed white above a forest of dark pines.

The freight train stretched almost one kilometer along a straight track. The freight train carried iron ore in sixty open wagons. The freight train ran between a desert mine and a coastal port. The freight train needed three diesel locomotives on the steepest grades.

The salt marsh covered a wide estuary between two low headlands. The salt marsh flooded twice a day under the incoming tide. The salt marsh supported samphire, sea lavender, and coarse grass. The salt marsh fed thousands of wading birds in winter.

The copper mine reached four hundred meters below a bare valley floor. The copper mine contained twelve kilometers of narrow tunnels. The copper mine stained the surrounding streams a bright orange. The copper mine employed most of the men in the valley.

The town clock tower rose thirty five meters above a cobbled square. The town clock tower held four white dials with black iron hands. The town clock tower contained a bronze bell of two tons. The town clock tower carried a weather vane in the shape of a fish.

The wooden canoe measured five meters from bow to stern. The wooden canoe weighed only twenty kilograms on dry land. The wooden canoe carried two paddlers and a week of supplies. The wooden canoe shone with many layers of clear varnish.

The herb garden filled a square plot behind a stone wall. The herb garden grew sage, thyme, mint, and lemon balm. The herb garden followed a plan of four beds around a sundial. The herb garden supplied the kitchen of a small guesthouse.

The seaside promenade ran two kilometers along a curving bay. The seaside promenade carried rows of blue and white beach huts. The seaside promenade held a bandstand near the eastern end. The seaside promenade offered wide views toward a distant island.

The granite statue stood four meters above a plinth of grey stone. The granite statue showed a fisherman with a coiled rope. The granite statue faced the harbor entrance and the open sea. The granite statue carried a bronze plaque with a short inscription.

The school gymnasium covered a full basketball court under a high roof. The school gymnasium held wooden wall bars along one long side. The school gymnasium served as an exam hall and a concert venue. The school gymnasium smelled of floor polish and old rubber mats.

The ski lift climbed six hundred meters to a bare ridge. The ski lift carried four skiers in each covered chair. The ski lift ran on a steel cable between eighteen pylons. The ski lift closed during strong winds from the north.

The pottery workshop occupied a converted stable beside a cobbled yard. The pottery workshop held four wheels and a large electric kiln. The pottery workshop sold plain bowls with a pale blue glaze. The pottery workshop taught evening classes for complete beginners.

The fishing village clung to a steep slope above a small harbor. The fishing village contained ninety houses of whitewashed stone. The fishing village lay eleven kilometers from the nearest main road. The fishing village kept a lifeboat in a shed near the pier.

The flour mill stood four floors high beside a wide stream. The flour mill ground wheat between two heavy French millstones. The flour mill stored grain in sacks on the upper floors. The flour mill served bakers from many nearby villages.

The puppet theater seated one hundred people in a narrow hall. The puppet theater kept six hundred carved puppets in a back room. The puppet theater performed old folk tales in a local dialect. The puppet theater stood in a quiet street near the cathedral.

The radio tower rose two hundred meters above a bare chalk hill. The radio tower stood on three legs of red and white steel. The radio tower carried antennas for radio and mobile networks. The radio tower flashed a red light for passing aircraft.

The apple orchard covered three hectares on a gentle southern slope. The apple orchard held two hundred trees of six old varieties. The apple orchard supplied a small cider press in the village. The apple orchard sheltered a flock of geese under the trees.

The children's hospital held one hundred and twenty beds across four wards. The children's hospital kept a school room for long stay patients. The children's hospital stood beside a park in the west of the city. The children's hospital employed nurses from many different countries.

The stone staircase counted three hundred worn steps of grey granite. The stone staircase climbed a steep cliff toward a small chapel. The stone staircase carried an iron handrail on the outer edge. The stone staircase filled with pilgrims during the summer months.

The beach hut measured three meters across a small wooden deck. The beach hut wore bright turquoise paint with white trim. The beach hut stood in a long row above a shingle beach. The beach hut held deckchairs, buckets, and a small gas stove.

The sculpture park covered forty hectares of woodland and rough pasture. The sculpture park displayed sixty large works in the open air. The sculpture park kept an indoor gallery in a former stable. The sculpture park allowed dogs on every marked path.

The glass factory stood beside a large bed of white sand. The glass factory ran three furnaces at fifteen hundred degrees. The glass factory produced bottles and window panes for the region. The glass factory employed six hundred workers from nearby villages.

The toy train set covered a plywood board of two square meters. The toy train set contained forty meters of narrow metal track. The toy train set held a station, a tunnel, and a small forest. The toy train set ran on a twelve volt transformer.

The university chapel seated two hundred people on plain oak benches. The university chapel held tall windows of clear leaded glass. The university chapel stood at the north end of a green quadrangle. The university chapel welcomed students of every faith.

The mountain pass reached two thousand one hundred meters between two peaks. The mountain pass linked a dry valley with a green plateau. The mountain pass carried a narrow road with dozens of hairpin bends. The mountain pass closed under deep snow for much of the winter.

The ancient mosaic covered the floor of a large Roman dining room. The ancient mosaic showed a sea god surrounded by leaping dolphins. The ancient mosaic used tiny cubes of limestone, marble, and glass. The ancient mosaic lay under a modern shelter of timber and glass.

The record shop filled a basement below a busy shopping street. The record shop stocked jazz, punk, and rare local pressings. The record shop kept two listening booths near the back wall. The record shop hosted live sets by young bands.

The peat bog covered eight hundred hectares of a flat basin. The peat bog reached a depth of six meters in the center. The peat bog preserved pollen and ancient timber in the wet layers. The peat bog supported sundews, cotton grass, and breeding curlews.

The tidal island lay one kilometer from a low sandy shore. The tidal island covered thirty hectares of grass and bare rock. The tidal island connected to the mainland by a stone causeway. The tidal island held a ruined chapel on the highest point.

The folk festival ran for three days on a wide riverside field. The folk festival hosted fiddlers, singers, and dancers from many countries. The folk festival drew about eight thousand visitors each summer. The folk festival kept a free stage for local schools.

The customs house presented a wide front of pale limestone. The customs house held tall arched windows above a stone quay. The customs house contained a great hall with a painted ceiling. The customs house stood at the heart of the old port district.

The wind farm covered a bare ridge above a rocky coast. The wind farm held twenty turbines with blades of sixty meters. The wind farm supplied electricity to a nearby coastal city. The wind farm shared the ridge with sheep and rough pasture.

The village school taught fifty pupils in three small classrooms. The village school occupied a Victorian building of red brick. The village school kept a vegetable plot behind the playground. The village school served six villages across a wide parish.

The old fortress crowned a rocky headland above a wide bay. The old fortress enclosed a courtyard behind walls of four meters. The old fortress kept a deep cistern under the central tower. The old fortress guarded the entrance to a natural harbor.

The cheese dairy produced a hard cheese from raw sheep milk. The cheese dairy occupied a stone building with thick cool walls. The cheese dairy aged wheels of cheese on long wooden shelves. The cheese dairy bought milk from four nearby farms.

The forest boardwalk ran two kilometers through a swampy pine forest. The forest boardwalk rested on short posts above black wet ground. The forest boardwalk carried information panels about mosses and frogs. The forest boardwalk allowed wheelchairs along the whole route.

The meteor crater showed a raised rim of shattered pale rock. The meteor crater measured twelve hundred meters across a flat desert plain. The meteor crater attracted geologists and curious tourists from many countries. The meteor crater reached a depth of one hundred and seventy meters.

The historic hotel contained ninety rooms behind a long stone facade. The historic hotel kept a ballroom with three crystal chandeliers. The historic hotel stood beside a busy railway station. The historic hotel served afternoon tea in a glass winter garden.
